title: Workspace Feature Tiers
goal: Implement #451 by defining compact feature tiers and activation criteria for major package capabilities.
non_goals: Do not build a large feature flag framework.
Do not remove useful features entirely.
Do not break current full installs without migration guidance.

intent.outcome: Major features have visible tier and activation metadata, and startup/report output can identify the active tier.
intent.constraints: Keep exact naming flexible and avoid broad framework work.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest contract/config/report changes that make tiers visible and useful.
intent.escalation: Escalate if tiering requires incompatible install semantics or a large feature-flag system.

execution.next_step: Inspect README, module registry, start output, report output, and #451 acceptance criteria.
execution.proof: workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, and planning doctor; broaden to package tests if package surfaces change

invariants: Full/dogfooding features are not implied defaults.
Tier metadata is compact and queryable.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #452-#460 plus #467/#469/#471 remain in the feature-tier and surface-merge lane.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #451 closeout, promote the next #450 child indicated by validation

parent_lane.title: Make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #452 or the next #450 child indicated by validation

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Define feature tiers and activation criteria for major package capabilities.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not build broad feature-flag infrastructure or imply full/dogfooding features by default.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact tier naming and metadata shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Tiering requires incompatible install behavior or a broad framework.

context_budget.live working set: state, this execplan, #450/#451, README, module registry, start/report outputs
context_budget.externalize before shift: record tier metadata and activation criteria plus any follow-up routing
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Next active issue is #451 feature tiers.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before changing install semantics or adding new config fields.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Make advanced capability cost visible and opt-in rather than implicit.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can a repo see and select the smallest viable tier without reading deep docs?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to feature-tier metadata and activation criteria.

immediate_next_action: Inspect README, module registry, start output, report output, and #451 acceptance criteria.
Define the smallest tier metadata and query/report surface that satisfies #451.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
Do not make advanced or dogfooding features implicit defaults.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Major features have visible tier/activation metadata.
Startup/report output can identify the active tier.
A repo can install only the smallest viable tier.
Full/dogfooding features are not implied defaults.

closure_check.evidence carried forward: #451 owns feature-tier metadata after #461 completed payload boundary work.
closure_check.reopen trigger: advanced/full/dogfooding capabilities remain implicit defaults or active tier is not visible in compact outputs

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Promoted after #466 completed the #461 payload-boundary lane.
